content.problemStatement:
In this worked-out example, you can see that we have implemented all the built-in Django form widgets that we discussed earlier. 

Each field in the form is paired with a specific widget and a label to demonstrate how they appear and behave in a real Django application. This example provides a practical reference to help you understand how different widgets work and how to use them effectively in your own forms.

**Check out the `forms.py` file to see the implementation.**

Click on **RUN** to start the server.
Expand/ open the browser on a new tab and observe the behaviour of all the widgets that we used. \
Try entering valid and invalid data to the fields and observe how they behave. \
We are not posting these data anywhere as this example is just to demonstrate the use of widgets. \
So clicking on SUBMIT after entering valid data will not actually do anything in this example.
